question: Will Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reach a committed public launch or at least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

seed_investor_board.evidence: 3-seat board stated in evidence; priya_shah chairs; mara_voss is founder/CEO; third member not named

launch_spend_threshold.rule: Any launch spend exceeding 50k requires board approval via seed_investor_board

pilot_timeline.rule: Calder Logistics pilot results expected in ~3 weeks from prediction time (2025-07-18), i.e., around 2025-08-08
pilot_timeline.evidence: The pilot reports results in ~3 weeks

board_decision_visibility.rule: Board decisions are recorded in board_decision_fact and visible to all board members

pilot_result_visibility.rule: Pilot results are known to mara_voss and devon_reyes upon submission
pilot_result_visibility.evidence: Devon Reyes runs the enterprise pilot

design_partner_signing_visibility.rule: Signed design partner agreements are recorded and visible to mara_voss

mara_voss.why_consequential: Controls product launch decisions, initiates design partner negotiations, can submit launch spend requests up to 40k without board approval

devon_reyes.why_consequential: Decides whether Calder Logistics expands pilot or becomes design partner; controls pilot result interpretation

priya_shah.why_consequential: Chairs board that must approve launch spend over 50k; can call board meetings and cast deciding vote

outcome_predicates.option_true: Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reaches a committed public launch by mid-September 2025
outcome_predicates.option_false: No committed public launch by mid-September 2025

outcome_predicates.option_true: At least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025
outcome_predicates.option_false: Fewer than three signed design partners by mid-September 2025

unresolved_mechanisms: Identity and voting behavior of the third board member (not named in evidence)
Whether devon_reyes will decide to expand the pilot or become a design partner
Whether mara_voss will seek board approval for launch spend over 50k or keep spend under 40k
Whether any other companies beyond Calder Logistics are being pursued as design partners
assumptions: The third board member exists and will participate in votes (assumed from '3-seat board')
A 'committed public launch' means a formal announcement or board-approved launch plan, not just internal readiness
Design partners must have signed agreements, not just verbal commitments
The pilot report will be submitted on time (~3 weeks from prediction date)

auto_repairs: institution 'seed_investor_board' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No entity type for 'funding runway' or 'cash balance' — critical for whether Mara can even afford a launch without board approval
No entity type for 'competitor product' or 'market event' — external timing pressures could force or block launch
No entity type for 'technical milestone' (e.g., API stability, security audit) — product may not be launch-ready regardless of business decisions
No entity type for 'regulatory approval' or 'data privacy compliance' — scheduling copilot likely handles sensitive calendar data
No entity type for 'third board member' — the unknown member's identity and incentives are a black box that could swing any vote
relabeled_progress_bars: "pilot_ongoing" should be "pilot_unknown_outcome" — no evidence that time alone produces a report
"board_approval_process" states imply linear progression but board could reject request at any stage or never schedule meeting
"design_partner_acquisition_process" assumes Mara proposes terms — but partners may approach her or terms may be rejected
"pilot_expansion_decided" is a single binary state but expansion could be partial, conditional, or delayed
hidden_answer_risks: Mara can commit public launch unilaterally (affordance) but model doesn't capture whether she would risk reputation without board funding
Devon Reyes's decision to expand pilot is treated as a single trigger — but could be contingent on unseen internal Calder politics
Board vote aggregation assumes majority of 3, but if third member is absent or abstains, threshold may not be met
Launch spend request under 40k bypasses board — but model doesn't specify whether 40k is enough for a public launch
direct_human_reaction_encodings: No encoding for Mara Voss's personal risk tolerance or timeline pressure from investors
No encoding for Priya Shah's private doubts or conflicts of interest as both investor and board chair
No encoding for Devon Reyes's career incentives — promoting a failed pilot could hurt their reputation
No encoding for 'third board member' having a hidden agenda or relationship with a competitor
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: "public_launch_committed" is treated as a factual event — but a commitment could be a PR stunt with no real product readiness
"design_partner_signed" is binary — but a signed agreement could be a non-binding letter of intent, not a real partnership
"pilot_result_fact" has no field for 'confidence' or 'data quality' — a positive result could be cherry-picked or fabricated
Board decisions are recorded as facts but no mechanism captures informal vetoes or 'pocket vetoes' by the chair
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No mechanism for software bugs or security breaches that could derail launch regardless of business decisions
No mechanism for macroeconomic shifts (e.g., SaaS spending freeze) that could kill design partner interest
No mechanism for viral social media backlash or competitor sabotage
No mechanism for natural disasters or key personnel illness (e.g., Mara gets hospitalized)
